task_spec.objective: Run all 5 Gateway atoms in sequence, carrying cross-step obligations, producing a coherent pre-processing result before Step 3 (Proceed).
constraints: Atoms must run in order: step0 → step1 → step1.5 → step2 → step2.5
Each atom's output feeds the next via carried_obligations
If any atom's validator fails, the macro logs the violation and proceeds with current behavior (no hard block — repair is incremental)
High-risk topic switch (step1) should warn before proceeding to step1.5+
success_conditions: Cross-step carried obligations are preserved (step0.depth/rigor → step2.5; step1.5.recommended_pack → step2; step2.predicted_skill → step2.5)
non_goals: Does not guarantee whole-companion reliability (paper non-claim)
Does not hard-block on validator failure (incremental repair model)
Does not replace instruction-based Gateway with a state machine

admissible: All atom outputs from the sequence
excluded: N/A (macro has access to all atom outputs)
unknown_state: Whether downstream Step 3+ will respect carried obligations

required_fields.name: atoms_executed
required_fields.validator: len == 5 and all steps present in order

required_fields.name: violations
required_fields.validator: each item is a {atom_id, violation_type, detail} dict

blocked_outputs: Proceeding to Step 3 when step1 detected high-risk topic switch without warning
step2 recommending a pack that step1.5 already recommended (duplicate)
step2.5 pausing on a non-evaluative query when step0.rigor=false

carried_obligations.rule: high risk should warn before proceeding
step2 must not re-recommend the same pack
if skill gap detected, don't redundantly pause

known_bad_fixtures.intended_reason: step1.5 and step2 both recommend deploy pack

on_violation.contract_revision: Add cross-step dedup to step2 memory_slice
Add sequence assertion to macro validator
